items.headline: City addresses affordability protections for subsidized housing projects
items.summary: The Council discussed new policies on City Affordability Restrictions for cases where project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This could impact the long-term affordability of housing units that currently serve low-income residents in San José.

items.headline: New residential development approved on Sherly Place
items.summary: Five new single-family homes permitted in the Sherly Place development (lots along Sherly Pl and Lincoln Ave), ranging from $447k-$570k. This represents a notable housing addition to the neighborhood with multiple units approved simultaneously.

items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 capital improvement spending plans
items.summary: Mountain View's city council held a study session to examine the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major infrastructure and development projects the city plans to fund. This is worth watching if you care about what physical projects—parks, roads, facilities—the city prioritizes in the coming year.

items.headline: City Council to Amend Tenant Protections Ordinance
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing changes to its Residential Tenant Protections Program (Chapter 19.71 of the Municipal Code). Residents should monitor this ordinance amendment as it could affect rental housing rights and landlord-tenant regulations in the city.

items.headline: City releases Children and Youth Services Master Plan status update
items.summary: The City Council reviewed progress on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan along with annual reports on youth programs including the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. This gives residents insight into how the city is planning and funding services for young people in San José.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan moves forward
items.summary: The Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, indicating ongoing work to improve San José's library system. This affects where residents can access public library services and what those facilities will look like.

items.headline: Downtown historic building seeks density bonus for renovation
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street is requesting elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of development in exchange for historic and seismic rehabilitation work. This decision could set a precedent for how the city incentivizes preservation of older buildings downtown.

items.headline: City of Cupertino launches summer jobs program for youth
items.summary: The city presented details on a new summer jobs initiative aimed at young residents. This program could provide employment opportunities for local teenagers and young adults during the summer months.
